title: Zona Luka
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Sith |image=[[File:Zonaluka.jpg]] |name=Zona Luka |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3996 BBY]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 4]] |species=[[Vultan]]Although Luka's species was never directly identified in [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]], she is depicted with the appearance of a Vultan in the comics, and the description of female Vultans in ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' matches her appearance. |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Green]] |skin=Yellow |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Brotherhood of the Sith]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] |masters=*[[Dominus]] {{C|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]}} *[[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] {{C|[[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]]}} |apprentices= }} {{Quote|The Jedi fight together!|Zona Luka|}} '''Zona Luka''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Vultan]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who was active during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. She was [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] ways by the respected [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Dominus]], who considered Luka his dearest [[Padawan/Legends|student]]. In [[3996 BBY]], Luka and a [[Brotherhood of the Sith|group]] of fellow Knights were visited on the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|library]]-[[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] by the Jedi [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], who claimed to be the keeper of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] knowledge forbidden by the Jedi Masters. Intrigued, Luka and the other Jedi agreed to join Kun on a journey to reveal the lost secrets of the Force. They traveled in Kun's [[Starship/Legends|starship]], ''[[Starstorm One]]'', to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]–tainted [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], and Luka and the others were led to the base of a [[Temple of Exar Kun|large temple]] by Kun. There, Kun revealed his true intentions when he shattered a [[Dark Holocron|Sith holocron]] and released the dark-side [[Force ghost/Legends|spirits]] trapped within, which possessed Luka and the other Jedi Knights. Kun, in reality the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], commanded Luka and the Jedi team—now subjects of the dark side—to [[Sith uprising|kill]] their former Masters. Luka tracked Dominus to [[Dominus' hut|his home]] and struck him down after a brief [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]]; however, the [[Death/Legends|dying]] Dominus mustered enough strength to kill the Vultan Jedi through an exertion of the Force. ==Biography== ===Meeting Exar Kun=== {{Quote|You're right. Arca failed miserably on ''Onderon''. I can't understand it. He was a great Jedi.|Zona Luka, to Exar Kun|}} [[File:JediOnOssus.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Zona Luka and a group of fellow Jedi speak with Exar Kun on Ossus.]] Zona Luka, a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Vultan]], was born in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. As a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Luka was admitted into the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to be [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Luka became the [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Dominus]], who considered the Vultan to be his dearest student. Under Dominus' guidance, Luka eventually became a full-fledged [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. In [[3996 BBY]], she was on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] when a Knight named [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] began to preach in the [[Gardens of T'alla/Legends|Gardens of T'alla]] regarding a renewal of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] way. Luka and a [[Brotherhood of the Sith|group]] of fellow Jedi, including the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Cay Qel-Droma]], fellow Vultan [[Oss Wilum]], and the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] [[Crado]], gathered in the Gardens to listen to Kun's words. Kun, in reality the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], claimed that he had discovered great secrets of the Force and would share them with any who joined him. He told the assembled Knights that the old Jedi Masters had failed to contain the influence of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], and Luka agreed, citing Master [[Arca Jeth]]'s [[Funeral of Amanoa|capture]] and [[Torture/Legends|torture]] by the [[Naddist]] [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]] on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. Her statement was supported by Kun, who proclaimed that he had destroyed the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of former Dark Lord of the Sith [[Freedon Nadd]], and had succeeded where the Jedi Masters had failed. When Qel-Droma challenged Kun to prove his boasts, Kun produced what he described as a [[Sith talisman/Legends|Jedi amulet]], which mesmerized Luka. As the assembled Knights stood transfixed by the amulet, Kun asked them to join him on a quest for the most ancient and powerful Jedi secrets, and promised that they could change [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] by strengthening the Jedi Order against the dark side. After a period of deliberation, Luka, Crado, Wilum, and the rest of the group who had heard Kun's sermon in the Gardens, excluding Qel-Droma, decided to join him on the trip and set out to inform Kun. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] they met him at the entrance to the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library's]] [[Chamber of Antiquities/Legends|Chamber of Antiquities]], Kun was in possession of the [[Draethos/Legends|Draethos]] Jedi Master [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr's]] [[Jedi apparel/Legends|robe]] and [[Staff/Legends|staff]]. Kun claimed that the ancient Jedi Master had [[Death/Legends|passed away]] peacefully—in reality, Kun had killed Odan-Urr after a short struggle—and revealed that Odan-Urr had promoted him to the rank of Master and given him a [[Dark Holocron|rare holocron]]. Luka and the other Jedi believed Kun's claims, even though he had lied about what had transpired. ===Fall to the dark side=== {{Quote|So far, all you've given us is a lot of talk, Exar Kun.|Zona Luka, on Yavin 4|}} [[File:JediNowSith.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Zona Luka is exposed to the spirits within the holocron.]] Luka and the other followers of Exar Kun boarded his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], ''[[Starstorm One]]'', and began their journey to learn the ancient mysteries of the Force. They traveled to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], which Kun promised was the home to many secrets and forgotten knowledge. The group of Jedi crossed a marshy area and eventually arrived at a [[Temple of Exar Kun|large temple]]. However, they grew impatient, and Luka asked Kun to finally prove his claims. Uncomfortable by the dark-side taint of Yavin 4, a nervous Oss Wilum attempted to leave the group and return to the ship. However, the Vultan Jedi found himself confronted by a large group of [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors—descendants of members of the warrior class of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]] who had come to the moon when Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] settled there in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]—and Luka and the other Jedi Knights sprang into action. The assembled Jedi banded together to fight the Massassi, until Kun commanded the natives to stop their attack. Determined to prove himself to Luka and his other would-be followers, Kun produced the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] that he had taken from Odan-Urr. He revealed it to be a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] holocron, not a Jedi holocron as the group of curious Knights previously believed, and asked Luka and the other Jedi to assist him in ridding Yavin 4 of the holocron's dark influence. Kun declared that he was going to destroy the device, and the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] instructed Luka's group to hold out their hands to block the flash caused by the [[Sith artifact/Legends|artifact's]] annihilation. However, unbeknownst to Luka and her Jedi brethren, Kun did not intend to destroy the holocron; instead, he planned to release the device's dark power. As Luka looked on, Kun crushed the holocron in his hand, releasing the Sith spirits trapped within. The shards of the dark holocron became embedded in Luka's skin, showing her the past glory of the Sith, and incorporated the teachings and [[Force power/Legends|powers]] of the Sith into the shocked Jedi. The fragments disappeared, and Luka was no longer a follower of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]]; she and the other Jedi who had accompanied her on the journey were now [[darksider]]s, possessed by the ghosts that inhabited the holocron. With Luka and her Jedi companions now under the influence of the dark side, Kun ordered them back to the ship. ===Death=== {{Quote|You were my dearest student, Zona Luka…"''
"''Nothing personal, Master Dominus…|Master Dominus and Zona Luka, shortly before their duel|}} [[File:ZonaVsDominus.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Zona Luka battles her former Master, Dominus.]] Kun commanded Luka and the other new dark-side followers to kill their former Masters, to make way for a new order in the galaxy.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]] Luka set out to find Dominus and tracked him down at [[Dominus' hut|his home]]. After a verbal exchange in a disheveled room, in which Luka professed that her attacking the Jedi was nothing personal, she and Dominus [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|dueled]] briefly with their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]]. Luka struck down the aging Master, but Dominus killed his fallen apprentice with the Force as he lay dying. After the battle, the scene was investigated by Knights [[Dace Diath]], [[Qrrrl Toq]], and [[Shoaneb Culu]], all of whom wondered why Luka would kill her former teacher. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|The Sith Empire must rise again… but the old Jedi Masters stand in our way.|Zona Luka|}} Zona Luka displayed curiosity toward the dark side and the forbidden secrets of the Force. Luka held doubts toward the teachings of the old Jedi Masters, a belief that Exar Kun played upon in convincing her to join him. Luka was also easily captivated by Kun's Sith amulet, showing her fascination with the Force's unknown. All of these factors contributed to Luka's decision to join Kun on his journey to reveal the Force's secrets, although she displayed impatience after Kun did not immediately back up his claims of hidden knowledge. Luka was very loyal to her fellow Jedi, exemplified by her reckless charge into battle with the Massassi to assist her friend Oss Wilum on Yavin 4. However, once she was exposed to the spirits inside the Dark Holocron, Luka changed, becoming willing to do anything, even betray the Jedi Order, for Kun. She began to see the old Jedi hierarchy as standing in the way of the rise of the [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] and therefore something that needed to be eliminated. This new Luka carried off her mission to kill her former Master without hesitation, although she assured Dominus that she still had no personal issue with him. ==Powers and abilities== Zona Luka was adept enough in the Force that Master Dominus considered her his favorite student, and Luka attained the rank of Jedi Knight. However, when she was turned to the dark side by the Dark Holocron, her powers in the Force increased even more, at least according to Exar Kun. Luka was skilled with the lightsaber, striking down Dominus and defending her fellow Jedi against a horde of Massassi. Luka possessed at least two different lightsabers: while on Yavin 4 she wielded a lightsaber with a [[Color/Legends|purple]] beam, while she utilized a blue one during her duel with Dominus. Despite her skill, she was not able to prevent the dying Dominus from killing her with the Force, her master blasting her with the [[Electric Judgment|lightning of the light side]] as he affirmed his vow to the same with his last breath. ==Behind the scenes== The character of Zona Luka first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]'', a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1995]]. Luka appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|first]], [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|second]], [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|third]], and [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|fourth]] issues of the arc and was killed in the fourth issue. In her appearances, Luka was illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 4]] {{1stID|Luka}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Zona Luka}} *{{InsiderCite|26|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 1}} *{{InsiderCite|28|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 3}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Zona Luka |fi=Zona Luka }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Luka, Zona}} [[Category:Fallen Jedi]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Members of the Sith Brotherhood]] [[Category:Pre-Ruusan Jedi Knights]] [[Category:Sith acolytes and apprentices]] [[Category:Vultans]]
